message:
fix(packs/strict_git): close refspec bypass on push to main/master

`git push origin HEAD:refs/heads/main` and `git push origin refs/heads/master`
were not matched by the push-main / push-master rules because the
separator before `main`/`master` was `/` — which the old `(?:.*[\s:])?`
didn't accept. Added `/` to the separator character class:

  (?:.*[\s:])?   ->  (?:.*[\s:/])?

The greedy `.*[\s:/]` still requires `main`/`master` to be preceded by
a real separator, so `feature-main`, `main-fix`, and `maintain` are
still allowed (no `-`, `.`, or alphanum before the branch name is
accepted as a separator).

Regression tests cover both explicit refspec forms for main and master.
